# veridigit

> Verified validation of structured identifiers (IBAN, payment cards, ISBN-13, VIN) for AI
> agents. Runs the real checksum algorithms instead of letting a model guess. Ships as an
> MCP server and a typed TypeScript library. By Qiniso — verified, trustworthy data tools
> for AI agents.

## What it does
- validate_iban: ISO 7064 mod-97 checksum + country-specific length (75+ countries).
- validate_card: Luhn (ISO/IEC 7812) checksum, brand detection from BIN, length rules.
- validate_isbn: ISBN-13 (978/979 prefix + mod-10 weighted check digit).
- validate_vin: ISO 3779 alphabet (no I/O/Q) + position-9 transliteration check digit.

## Why
On arbitrary (non-memorised) identifiers, a frontier LLM with no tool answered check-digit
questions wrong ~91% of the time, and silently — confident, well-formatted, wrong.
veridigit is correct 100% of the time because it runs the algorithm.

## Scope
Validates the STRUCTURE (format + checksum) of an identifier. Does NOT confirm a real,
active, or owned account/card/book/vehicle. No network calls.

## Install
- MCP: npx -y veridigit  (tools: validate_iban, validate_card, validate_isbn, validate_vin)
- Library: npm install veridigit

## Links
- Repo: https://github.com/qinisolabs/veridigit
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/veridigit
